title: Manufacturing | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
body: Breadcrumb MIT News Topics Manufacturing Topic Manufacturing Download RSS feed: News Articles / In the Media / Audio Tackling the housing shortage with robotic microfactories Reframe Systems, co-founded by Vikas Enti SM '20, creates microfactories for modular home construction, reducing carbon emissions and costs. April 21, 2026 Read full story → How to expand the US economy In “Priority Technologies,” MIT faculty examine key areas of innovation that can drive American prosperity and security — now and in the decades ahead. April 21, 2026 Read full story → MIT researchers use AI to uncover atomic defects in materials A new model measures defects that can be leveraged to improve materials’ mechanical strength, heat transfer, and energy-conversion efficiency. March 30, 2026 Read full story → Active Surfaces aims to install peel-and-stick solar panels everywhere This award-winning startup with roots at the MIT Energy Initiative is developing lightweight, flexible, high-efficiency solar energy films designed to be used on roofs, walls, and any curved surface. March 24, 2026 Read full story → Tackling industry’s burdensome bubble problem MIT researchers uncovered the physics behind bubble-removing membranes that could improve bioreactors, chemical production, and more. February 26, 2026 Read full story → Welcome to the “most wicked” apprentice program on campus With a focus on metallurgy and fabrication, Pappalardo Apprentices assist their peers with machining, hand-tool use, brainstorming, and more, while expanding their own skills. January 28, 2026 Read full story → Introducing the Minerals Stewardship Consortium at MIT The consortium convenes industry, academia, and policy leaders to navigate competing demands and reimagine materials supply. December 16, 2025 Read full story → MIT takes manufacturing education across the country The new TechAMP program teaches production principles to workers, helping them advance their careers and identify savings at their firms. December 10, 2025 Read full story → MIT NEET students install solar-powered charging station on campus, resurrecting an MIT tradition The project was designed and built with novel “bio-composite” materials developed by the student team. December 9, 2025 Read full story → From nanoscale to global scale: Advancing MIT’s special initiatives in manufacturing, health, and climate MIT.nano cleanroom complex named after Robert Noyce PhD ’53 at the 2025 Nano Summit. November 13, 2025 Read full story → Battery-powered appliances make it easy to switch from gas to electric Founded by Sam Calisch SM ’14, PhD ’19, Copper offers electric kitchen ranges that plug into standard wall outlets, with no electrical upgrades required. October 30, 2025 Read full story → MIT engineers solve the sticky-cell problem in bioreactors and other industries Their system uses electrochemically generated bubbles to detach cells from surfaces, which could accelerate the growth of carbon-absorbing algae and lifesaving cell therapies. October 15, 2025 Read full story → Checking the quality of materials just got easier with a new AI tool Acting as a “virtual spectrometer,” SpectroGen generates spectroscopic data in any modality, such as X-ray or infrared, to quickly assess a material’s quality. October 14, 2025 Read full story → MIT gears up to transform manufacturing The Initiative for New Manufacturing is convening experts across the Institute to drive a transformation of production across the U.S. and the world. August 13, 2025 Read full story → Harvey Kent Bowen, ceramics scholar and MIT Leaders for Global Operations co-founder, dies at 83 Bowen’s innovative work helped transform ceramics and manufacturing education at MIT and beyond. August 11, 2025 Read full story →
